mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in retail sales signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2017 vintage RSAFS was 473808 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative 3-month RSAFS growth, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions; when FEDFUNDS crosses upward by at least 0.25 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises >= 0.25pp over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2017 vintage FEDFUNDS was 0.91 percent during a tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at t-test p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening labor income and recession risk; when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or if triggered mean returns are not lower than non-triggered mean returns at p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with rising policy rates creates a valuation headwind: when CPIAUCSL growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS rises together, tighter real and nominal discount-rate expectations drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth increases versus its prior 3-month rate and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2017 vintage CPIAUCSL was 243.846 and FEDFUNDS was 0.91
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase trigger has no incremental negative effect versus either single-series trigger, measured by an interaction coefficient with p > 0.10 or by lower out-of-sample accuracy than the single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth indicates improving output, capacity utilization, and earnings momentum; positive INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 forward returns than negative 6-month INDPRO growth, or if the sign of the estimated coefficient is negative in two consecutive walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction-related income; when HOUST drops well below its recent trend, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:April 2017 vintage HOUST was 1172 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside threshold events are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if their average return is not below the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll expansion with low unemployment confirms broad income growth without immediate labor-market stress; when PAYEMS rises briskly while UNRATE remains low or falling, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change > 150 thousand and UNRATE does not rise over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2017 vintage PAYEMS was 146135 thousand and UNRATE was 4.3 percent
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor conditions
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE joint trigger does not outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 positive-return frequency, or if the interaction term fails to add predictive power versus PAYEMS alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation during stable policy reduces discount-rate pressure without signaling immediate recession; when CPIAUCSL inflation momentum falls and FEDFUNDS is unchanged, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth declines versus its prior 3-month rate and FEDFUNDS changes < 0.05pp over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if stable-policy disinflation triggers have lower 5-day S&P 500 mean returns than non-triggered observations, or if positive-return frequency after triggers is not above the unconditional baseline.
